label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out step by step. So there's a gift exchange with five people: Alice, Bob, Claire, Dave, and Eve. Each starts with a different colored present. The problem is about tracking who ends up with which present after a series of swaps. The question is asking what present Eve has at the end, and the options are given. First, let me list out the initial gifts: - Alice: yellow - Bob: orange ball - Claire: red - Dave: purple - Eve: green Now, there are several swaps. Let me go through each one in order and track who has what after each step. First swap: Alice and Eve swap. So Alice gives her yellow present to Eve, and Eve gives her green present to Alice. After this swap: - Alice: green - Eve: yellow - Bob: orange (still) - Claire: red (still) - Dave: purple (still) Second swap: Bob and Dave swap. Bob has orange, Dave has purple. After swapping: - Bob: purple - Dave: orange Others remain the same: - Alice: green - Eve: yellow - Claire: red Third swap: Bob and Eve swap. Now Bob has purple, Eve has yellow. After swapping: - Bob: yellow - Eve: purple Others: - Alice: green - Dave: orange - Claire: red Fourth swap: Claire and Eve swap. Claire has red, Eve has purple. After swapping: - Claire: purple - Eve: red Others: - Alice: green - Bob: yellow - Dave: orange Fifth swap: Dave and Alice swap. Dave has orange, Alice has green. After swapping: - Dave: green - Alice: orange Others: - Bob: yellow - Claire: purple - Eve: red So at the end, Eve has the red present. The options are (C) red present. Let me check again each step to make sure I didn't make a mistake. First swap Alice-Eve: Alice green, Eve yellow. Correct. Second swap Bob-Dave: Bob purple, Dave orange. Correct. Third swap Bob-Eve: Bob now has yellow, Eve has purple. Correct. Fourth swap
